title: Merf
content:
{{Top|leg}} {{Character |type=Criminal |image=[[File:Merf.jpg]] |name=Merf |homeworld=[[Vorzyd V/Legends|Vorzyd V]]"[[Gambler's World]]" |birth= |death= |species=[[Vorzydiak]] |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Male]] |pronouns=new |height= |mass= |hair=Gray |feathers= |eyes= |skin= |cyber= |families=new |parents=new |partners=new |siblings=new |children=new |affiliation=[[Freelies]] |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|Let's get outta here... before we get ''blamed'' for this mess!|Merf|}} '''Merf''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Vorzydiak]] member of the [[Freelies]] gang on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Vorzyd V/Legends|Vorzyd V]] during the era of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. A loyal member of the Freelies, Merf participated in a daring ransom scheme in [[1 ABY/Legends|1 ABY]], when his gang kidnapped prominent [[Alliance to Restore the Republic|Rebel]] leader [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]] and attempted to sell her back to the Alliance for a large sum. However, a squad of [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]] soon stormed the Freelies' hideout, Organa escaped in the firefight and Merf and his friends ended up with nothing. ==Biography== {{Quote|The Empire doesn't even know we exist! Nobody does!"
"...And that's the way we like it!|Jax and Merf|}} A [[Gender|male]] [[Vorzydiak]], Merf was a native of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]]{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}} [[Planet/Legends|world]] [[Vorzyd V/Legends|Vorzyd V]], better known by the moniker "the Gambler's World." By [[1 ABY/Legends|1 ABY]], Merf had become a member of the [[Freelies]], a delinquent youth gang led by a [[Human/Legends|Human]] named [[Jax (Freelie)|Jax]]. Merf and his friends were known to patrol the dingy streets of the [[Capital/Legends|capital]] [[City/Legends|city]] of [[Efavan/Legends|Efavan]] looking for scores, and one night they happened across a pair of valuable-looking [[Droid/Legends|droids]] wandering alone. Although Merf and the gang attempted to claim the droids, [[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO]] and [[R2-D2]], for themselves, they quickly realized that they had forgotten the tool they needed to remove C-3PO's [[Restraining bolt/Legends|restraining bolt]], which gave R2-D2 the time it needed to call a [[Police droid/Legends|police droid]] which chased the Freelies off. [[File:MerfSurrounded.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Merf realizes the Freelie den is surrounded by stormtroopers.]] However, Merf and his friends soon encountered those same droids again, after the Freelies entered a weapons shop intending to pull a caper. The droids were trying on automatic defensive weapons, which soon went haywire and damaged the store, convincing the Freelies that they'd be better served keeping their distance. Instead, they hung back and followed the droids to a [[Luke Skywalker's starship (Tatooine)|starship]] belonging to their owner, [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] hero [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]]. There, the Freelies tricked C-3PO into letting them aboard and kidnapped prominent Rebel leader [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]], bringing her back to their hideout and demanding five million [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]] in exchange for the Princess' safe return to the Alliance. However, a significant wrench was thrown in the Freelies' plans when Bemmie was captured by [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]] also looking for Organa. Bemmie was rescued from brutal [[Torture/Legends|torture]] by Skywalker, and the grateful Vorzydiak led Skywalker back to the Freelie hideout. Although Jax refused to trust Skywalker, the two groups were briefly forced to take up arms together when the hideout was stormed by a squad of stormtroopers. Jax planned to sell Skywalker and Organa once he managed to contact the troops' leader, but Bemmie led the Rebels to a secret passage out of the hideout, freeing them from the schemes of Merf and the Freelies. ==Personality and traits== {{Quote|A droid... two droids... all our own-!|Merf|}} Merf was a loyal foot soldier in the Freelies gang, obediently following Jax's commands and subscribing to the group's philosophy that the most important thing was that the Freelies be left to their own devices. Merf coveted the two droids C-3PO and R2-D2, and was hardly able to fathom the idea of actually having two droids to call his own. ==Behind the scenes== Merf made his first and only appearance in the [[Star Wars Legends|''Star Wars'' Legends]] continuity in ''[[Gambler's World]]'', a [[Star Wars (comic strip)|comic strip]] story arc written and illustrated by [[Russ Manning]] and released in [[1979]]. At first, Merf is mistakenly identified as fellow Vorzydiak Bemmie, but that mistake is rectified later in the story. ==Appearances== *"[[Gambler's World]]" {{Reprint|Gambler's World}} {{1st}} ==Sources== *{{CSWECite|II|307|Merf}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |nl=Merf }} [[Category:Freelies]] [[Category:Males]] [[Category:Vorzydiaks]]
